texto: The excitable observer will pass judgement first and then make knowledge conform to judgement the prudent observer will first learn to know and then judge according to knowledge.
explicacao: Citação de Thomas Cleary sobre temas ligados a sabedoria. “The excitable observer will pass judgement first and then make knowledge conform to judgement the…” articula sabedoria, judgement, knowledge em registro de sabedoria, com relevância para contextos como knowledge e observer.

texto: When flowing water...meets with obstacles on its path, a blockage in its journey, it pauses. It increases in volume and strength, filling up in front of the obstacle and eventually spilling past it...Do not turn and run, for there is nowhere worthwhile for you to go. Do not attempt to push ahead into the danger... emulate the example of the water: Pause and build up your strength until the obstacle no longer represents a blockage.
explicacao: Citação de Thomas Cleary sobre esforço, persistência e resultados. “When flowing water...meets with obstacles on its path, a blockage in its journey, it pauses. It i…” articula reflexao, spirituality, water em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como viagem e estudos.

texto: The Buddha is like space, with no inherent nature; appearing in the world to benefit the living, his features and refinements are like reflections.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Thomas Cleary: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a vida. O trecho “The Buddha is like space, with no inherent nature; appearing in the world to benefit the living, …” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que vida, buddha, buddhism pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de buddhism e nature.

texto: What worlds are there herein? I’ll tell you. In these seas of fragrant waters, numerous as atoms in unspeakably many buddha-fields, rest an equal number of world systems. Each world system also contains an equal number of worlds. Those world systems in the ocean of worlds have various resting places, various shapes and forms, various substances and essences, various locations, various entryways, various adornments, various boundaries, various alignments, various similarities, and various powers of maintenance.
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Thomas Cleary que provoca temas ligados a reflexao. Ao questionar “What worlds are there herein? I’ll tell you. In these seas of fragrant waters, numerous as atoms …”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre reflexao, buddha-fields, buddhism, com foco em buddhism e flower-ornament.
